title: Getting Started with omophub
text: omophub package provides R interface OMOPHub API accessing OHDSI ATHENA standardized medical vocabularies. vignette demonstrates basic usage patterns.

title: Getting Started with omophub
text: Install GitHub:
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("omophub/omophub-R")

title: Getting Started with omophub
text: package requires API key OMOPHub. Set API key environment variable: add .Renviron file persistence:
code: Sys.setenv(OMOPHUB_API_KEY = "your_api_key_here") OMOPHUB_API_KEY=your_api_key_here

title: Getting Started with omophub
code: library(omophub)  # Create client (reads API key from environment) client <- OMOPHubClient$new()  # Or provide API key explicitly client <- OMOPHubClient$new(api_key = "your_api_key")  # With additional options client <- OMOPHubClient$new(   api_key = "your_api_key",   timeout = 30,   max_retries = 3,   vocab_version = "2025.1" )

title: Getting Started with omophub
text: Retrieve concept OMOP concept ID: Get concept vocabulary-specific code:
code: concept <- client$concepts$get(201826) print(concept$concept_name) # [1] "Type 2 diabetes mellitus" concept <- client$concepts$get_by_code("SNOMED", "44054006") print(concept$concept_name) # [1] "Type 2 diabetes mellitus"

title: Getting Started with omophub
text: Retrieve multiple concepts single request:
code: result <- client$concepts$batch(c(201826, 4329847, 1112807)) for (concept in result$concepts) {   cat(sprintf("%s: %s\n", concept$concept_id, concept$concept_name)) }

title: Getting Started with omophub
text: Basic search: Search filters:
code: results <- client$search$basic("diabetes mellitus", page_size = 10) for (concept in results$data) {   cat(sprintf("%s: %s\n", concept$concept_id, concept$concept_name)) } results <- client$search$basic(   "heart attack",   vocabulary_ids = "SNOMED",   domain_ids = "Condition",   page_size = 20 )

title: Getting Started with omophub
text: Search using natural language queries powered neural embeddings: Filter semantic search results: Fetch semantic search results automatic pagination:
code: # Natural language search - understands clinical intent results <- client$search$semantic("high blood sugar levels") for (r in results$data$results) {   cat(sprintf("%s (similarity: %.2f)\n", r$concept_name, r$similarity_score)) } results <- client$search$semantic(   "heart attack",   vocabulary_ids = "SNOMED",   domain_ids = "Condition",   threshold = 0.5 ) all_results <- client$search$semantic_all(   "chronic kidney disease",   page_size = 50,   max_pages = 5,   progress = TRUE ) print(nrow(all_results))

title: Getting Started with omophub
text: Find concepts similar reference concept: Search natural language query different algorithms:
code: # Find concepts similar to Type 2 diabetes mellitus similar <- client$search$similar(concept_id = 201826) for (s in similar$similar_concepts) {   cat(sprintf("%s (score: %.2f)\n", s$concept_name, s$similarity_score)) } # Semantic similarity (neural embeddings) similar <- client$search$similar(   query = "high blood pressure",   algorithm = "semantic" )  # Lexical similarity (string matching) similar <- client$search$similar(   query = "high blood pressure",   algorithm = "lexical" )  # Hybrid (combined - default) similar <- client$search$similar(   query = "high blood pressure",   algorithm = "hybrid",   include_scores = TRUE,   include_explanations = TRUE )

title: Getting Started with omophub
text: Search multiple queries single API call — much faster individual requests many terms look .

title: Getting Started with omophub
text: Execute 50 keyword searches :
code: results <- client$search$bulk_basic(list(   list(search_id = "q1", query = "diabetes mellitus"),   list(search_id = "q2", query = "hypertension"),   list(search_id = "q3", query = "aspirin") ), defaults = list(vocabulary_ids = list("SNOMED"), page_size = 5))  # Each result is matched by search_id for (item in results$results) {   cat(sprintf("%s: %d results\n", item$search_id, length(item$results))) }

title: Getting Started with omophub
text: Execute 25 natural-language searches using neural embeddings: Defaults apply searches; per-search values override :
code: results <- client$search$bulk_semantic(list(   list(search_id = "s1", query = "heart failure treatment options"),   list(search_id = "s2", query = "type 2 diabetes medication") ), defaults = list(threshold = 0.5, page_size = 10))  for (item in results$results) {   cat(sprintf("%s: %d results\n", item$search_id,               item$result_count %||% length(item$results))) } # Different domains per query, shared vocabulary filter results <- client$search$bulk_basic(list(   list(search_id = "cond", query = "diabetes", domain_ids = list("Condition")),   list(search_id = "drug", query = "metformin", domain_ids = list("Drug")) ), defaults = list(vocabulary_ids = list("SNOMED", "RxNorm"), page_size = 5))

title: Getting Started with omophub
text: Get suggestions autocomplete:
code: suggestions <- client$concepts$suggest("diab", page_size = 5) for (s in suggestions$suggestions) {   print(s$suggestion) }

title: Getting Started with omophub
code: # First page results <- client$search$basic("diabetes", page = 1, page_size = 50)  # Check pagination info print(results$meta$total_items) print(results$meta$has_next)  # Get next page if available if (isTRUE(results$meta$has_next)) {   page2 <- client$search$basic("diabetes", page = 2, page_size = 50) }

title: Getting Started with omophub
text: Fetch results tibble:
code: all_results <- client$search$basic_all(   "diabetes",   page_size = 100,   max_pages = 5,   progress = TRUE )  # Results are a tibble print(nrow(all_results)) print(names(all_results))

title: Getting Started with omophub
text: Get ancestors (parent concepts): Get descendants (child concepts):
code: result <- client$hierarchy$ancestors(201826, max_levels = 3) for (ancestor in result$ancestors) {   print(ancestor$concept_name) } result <- client$hierarchy$descendants(201826, max_levels = 2) for (descendant in result$descendants) {   print(descendant$concept_name) }

title: Getting Started with omophub
text: Find concept maps vocabularies: Map specific vocabularies:
code: result <- client$mappings$get(201826) for (mapping in result$mappings) {   cat(sprintf("%s: %s\n",               mapping$target_vocabulary_id,               mapping$target_concept_name)) } result <- client$mappings$get(   201826,   target_vocabulary = "ICD10CM" )

title: Getting Started with omophub
text: Use tryCatch handle errors:
code: tryCatch(   {     concept <- client$concepts$get(999999999)   },   omophub_not_found = function(e) {     message("Concept not found: ", e$message)   },   omophub_api_error = function(e) {     message("API error: ", e$message)   } )

title: Getting Started with omophub
text: FHIR resolver translates FHIR coded values OMOP standard concepts single call — handling URI mapping, code lookup, Maps traversal, CDM target table assignment automatically.

title: Getting Started with omophub
code: result <- client$fhir$resolve(   system = "http://snomed.info/sct",   code = "44054006",   resource_type = "Condition" ) cat(result$resolution$standard_concept$concept_name) cat(result$resolution$target_table)   # "condition_occurrence" cat(result$resolution$mapping_type)   # "direct"

title: Getting Started with omophub
code: result <- client$fhir$resolve(   system = "http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm",   code = "E11.9" ) cat(result$resolution$mapping_type)                    # "mapped" cat(result$resolution$standard_concept$vocabulary_id)  # "SNOMED"

title: Getting Started with omophub
code: batch <- client$fhir$resolve_batch(list(   list(system = "http://snomed.info/sct", code = "44054006"),   list(system = "http://loinc.org", code = "2339-0") )) cat(sprintf("Resolved: %d/%d\n", batch$summary$resolved, batch$summary$total))

title: Getting Started with omophub
code: result <- client$fhir$resolve_codeable_concept(   coding = list(     list(system = "http://snomed.info/sct", code = "44054006"),     list(system = "http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm", code = "E11.9")   ),   resource_type = "Condition" ) # SNOMED wins over ICD-10-CM per OHDSI preference cat(result$best_match$resolution$source_concept$vocabulary_id)  # "SNOMED"

title: Getting Started with omophub
text: dplyr / tidyr workflows, pass as_tibble = TRUE get flat tibble one row per input coding columns source standard concepts, target CDM table, mapping type, resolution status. ergonomic shape ETL pipelines processing many codes: Failed rows kept -place status = "failed" error text status_detail — can filter rather silently dropping . batch summary (total / resolved / failed) attached attribute: default as_tibble = FALSE still returns list-shaped list(results, summary), existing code keeps working unchanged.
code: library(dplyr)  tbl <- client$fhir$resolve_batch(   list(     list(system = "http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm", code = "E11.9"),     list(system = "http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm", code = "I10"),     list(system = "http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm", code = "J45.909")   ),   as_tibble = TRUE )  tbl |>   filter(status == "resolved") |>   select(source_code, standard_concept_name, target_table) attr(tbl, "summary")

title: Getting Started with omophub
text: pipe-friendly workflows, omophub also exports standalone wrapper functions take client first argument. forms fully supported — pick whichever reads better surrounding code:
code: # Equivalent to client$fhir$resolve() client |>   fhir_resolve(     system = "http://snomed.info/sct",     code = "44054006",     resource_type = "Condition"   )  # Tibble-shaped batch in a pipe tbl <- client |>   fhir_resolve_batch(     codings = list(       list(system = "http://snomed.info/sct", code = "44054006"),       list(system = "http://loinc.org", code = "2339-0")     ),     as_tibble = TRUE   )  client |>   fhir_resolve_codeable_concept(     coding = list(       list(system = "http://snomed.info/sct", code = "44054006"),       list(system = "http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm", code = "E11.9")     ),     resource_type = "Condition"   )

title: Getting Started with omophub
text: need raw FHIR Parameters / Bundle responses instead Concept Resolver envelope, omophub_fhir_url() returns OMOPHub FHIR Terminology Service base URL can talk directly httr2 fhircrackr. Supports FHIR versions "r4" (default), "r4b", "r5", "r6": Example: call CodeSystem/$lookup directly httr2: Use client$fhir$resolve() (fhir_resolve()) want OMOP-enriched answers (standard concept, CDM target table, mapping quality). Use omophub_fhir_url() + httr2 need FHIR-native responses FHIR-native tooling.
code: omophub_fhir_url() #> "https://fhir.omophub.com/fhir/r4"  omophub_fhir_url("r5") #> "https://fhir.omophub.com/fhir/r5" library(httr2)  resp <- request(omophub_fhir_url()) |>   req_url_path_append("CodeSystem/$lookup") |>   req_url_query(     system = "http://snomed.info/sct",     code = "44054006"   ) |>   req_headers(Authorization = paste("Bearer", Sys.getenv("OMOPHUB_API_KEY"))) |>   req_perform()  params <- resp_body_json(resp) # Raw FHIR Parameters resource with the concept display and designations.

title: R Client for the OMOPHub Medical Vocabulary API
text: Query millions standardized medical concepts R Access SNOMED CT, ICD-10, RxNorm, LOINC, 90+ OHDSI ATHENA vocabularies without downloading, installing, maintaining local databases.  Documentation · API Reference · Examples

title: R Client for the OMOPHub Medical Vocabulary API
text: Working OHDSI ATHENA vocabularies traditionally requires downloading multi-gigabyte files, setting database, writing complex SQL queries. OMOPHub eliminates friction.

title: R Client for the OMOPHub Medical Vocabulary API
code: # Install from CRAN install.packages("omophub")  # Or install development version from GitHub # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("omopHub/omophub-R")

title: R Client for the OMOPHub Medical Vocabulary API
text: Get API key OMOPHub Dashboard.
code: library(omophub)  # Option 1: Environment variable (recommended) Sys.setenv(OMOPHUB_API_KEY = "oh_xxxxxxxxx")  # Option 2: Use helper function set_api_key("oh_xxxxxxxxx")  # Option 3: Store securely in system keyring set_api_key("oh_xxxxxxxxx", store = "keyring")

title: R Client for the OMOPHub Medical Vocabulary API
code: library(omophub)  # Create client client <- OMOPHubClient$new()  # Get a concept by ID concept <- client$concepts$get(201826) concept$concept_name # [1] "Type 2 diabetes mellitus"  # Search for concepts results <- client$search$basic("metformin", vocabulary_ids = "RxNorm") results$data  # Get concept by vocabulary code snomed_concept <- client$concepts$get_by_code("SNOMED", "44054006")  # Map to another vocabulary mappings <- client$mappings$get(201826, target_vocabulary = "ICD10CM")  # Navigate hierarchy ancestors <- client$hierarchy$ancestors(201826, max_levels = 3)

title: R Client for the OMOPHub Medical Vocabulary API
text: Use natural language queries find concepts using neural embeddings:
code: # Natural language search - understands clinical intent results <- client$search$semantic("high blood sugar levels") for (r in results$data$results) {   cat(sprintf("%s (similarity: %.2f)\n", r$concept_name, r$similarity_score)) }  # Filter by vocabulary and set minimum similarity threshold results <- client$search$semantic(   "heart attack",   vocabulary_ids = "SNOMED",   domain_ids = "Condition",   threshold = 0.5 )  # Fetch all results with auto-pagination all_results <- client$search$semantic_all("chronic kidney disease", page_size = 50)  # Find concepts similar to a reference concept similar <- client$search$similar(concept_id = 201826, algorithm = "hybrid") for (s in similar$similar_concepts) {   cat(sprintf("%s (score: %.2f)\n", s$concept_name, s$similarity_score)) }

title: R Client for the OMOPHub Medical Vocabulary API
text: Search multiple terms single API call:
code: # Bulk lexical search (up to 50 queries) results <- client$search$bulk_basic(list(   list(search_id = "q1", query = "diabetes mellitus"),   list(search_id = "q2", query = "hypertension"),   list(search_id = "q3", query = "aspirin") ), defaults = list(vocabulary_ids = list("SNOMED"), page_size = 5))  for (item in results$results) {   cat(sprintf("%s: %d results\n", item$search_id, length(item$results))) }  # Bulk semantic search (up to 25 queries) results <- client$search$bulk_semantic(list(   list(search_id = "s1", query = "heart failure treatment options"),   list(search_id = "s2", query = "type 2 diabetes medication") ), defaults = list(threshold = 0.5, page_size = 10))

title: R Client for the OMOPHub Medical Vocabulary API
text: Resolve FHIR coded values OMOP standard concepts one call:
code: # Single FHIR Coding -> OMOP concept + CDM target table result <- client$fhir$resolve(   system = "http://snomed.info/sct",   code = "44054006",   resource_type = "Condition" ) result$resolution$target_table # [1] "condition_occurrence"  # ICD-10-CM -> traverses 'Maps to' automatically result <- client$fhir$resolve(   system = "http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm",   code = "E11.9" ) result$resolution$standard_concept$vocabulary_id # [1] "SNOMED"  # Batch resolve up to 100 codings batch <- client$fhir$resolve_batch(list(   list(system = "http://snomed.info/sct", code = "44054006"),   list(system = "http://loinc.org", code = "2339-0"),   list(system = "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm", code = "197696") )) cat(sprintf("Resolved %d/%d\n", batch$summary$resolved, batch$summary$total))  # CodeableConcept with vocabulary preference (SNOMED wins over ICD-10) result <- client$fhir$resolve_codeable_concept(   coding = list(     list(system = "http://snomed.info/sct", code = "44054006"),     list(system = "http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm", code = "E11.9")   ),   resource_type = "Condition" ) result$best_match$resolution$source_concept$vocabulary_id # [1] "SNOMED"

title: R Client for the OMOPHub Medical Vocabulary API
text: Validate map clinical codes OMOP CDM transformations:
code: # Validate source codes and find standard equivalents validate_and_map <- function(source_vocab, source_code) {   concept <- client$concepts$get_by_code(source_vocab, source_code)      if (concept$standard_concept != "S") {     mappings <- client$mappings$get(       concept$concept_id,       target_vocabulary = "SNOMED"     )     return(mappings$mappings[[1]]$target_concept_id)   }      concept$concept_id }  # Example: Map ICD-10 to SNOMED standard_id <- validate_and_map("ICD10CM", "E11.9")

title: R Client for the OMOPHub Medical Vocabulary API
text: Verify codes exist valid:
code: # Check if condition codes are valid condition_codes <- c("E11.9", "I10", "J44.9")  for (code in condition_codes) {   tryCatch({     concept <- client$concepts$get_by_code("ICD10CM", code)     message(sprintf("OK %s: %s", code, concept$concept_name))   }, omophub_api_error = function(e) {     message(sprintf("ERROR %s: Invalid code!", code))   }) }

title: R Client for the OMOPHub Medical Vocabulary API
text: Explore hierarchies build comprehensive concept sets:
code: # Get all descendants for phenotype definition descendants <- client$hierarchy$descendants(   201826,  # Type 2 diabetes mellitus   max_levels = 5 )  concept_set <- sapply(descendants$concepts, function(x) x$concept_id) message(sprintf("Found %d concepts for T2DM phenotype", length(concept_set)))

title: R Client for the OMOPHub Medical Vocabulary API
code: library(dplyr) library(purrr)  # Search and convert to tibble results <- client$search$basic("hypertension", page_size = 100)  concepts_df <- results$data %>%   map_dfr(~ tibble(     concept_id = .x$concept_id,     concept_name = .x$concept_name,     vocabulary_id = .x$vocabulary_id,     domain_id = .x$domain_id   ))  # Filter and analyze concepts_df %>%   filter(vocabulary_id == "SNOMED") %>%   count(domain_id)

title: R Client for the OMOPHub Medical Vocabulary API
code: # Automatic pagination - fetch all results all_results <- client$search$basic_all("diabetes", page_size = 100)  # Manual pagination page1 <- client$search$basic("diabetes", page = 1, page_size = 20) page2 <- client$search$basic("diabetes", page = 2, page_size = 20)

title: R Client for the OMOPHub Medical Vocabulary API
code: # Specify vocabulary version client <- OMOPHubClient$new(vocab_version = "2025.2")  # Custom configuration client <- OMOPHubClient$new(   api_key = "oh_xxx",   base_url = "https://api.omophub.com/v1",   timeout = 30 )

title: R Client for the OMOPHub Medical Vocabulary API
code: tryCatch({   result <- client$concepts$get(999999999) }, omophub_not_found_error = function(e) {   message("Concept not found: ", conditionMessage(e)) }, omophub_auth_error = function(e) {   message("Check your API key") }, omophub_rate_limit_error = function(e) {   message("Rate limited, please wait") }, omophub_api_error = function(e) {   message("API error: ", conditionMessage(e)) })

title: omophub 1.7.0
text: Tibble output batch FHIR resolution FhirResource$resolve_batch() (standalone fhir_resolve_batch()) gained as_tibble parameter. as_tibble = TRUE, call returns flat tibble::tibble one row per input coding columns source concept, standard concept, target CDM table, mapping type, resolution status - ready pipe dplyr / tidyr. batch summary (total / resolved / failed) attached attr(result, "summary"). Default behavior unchanged: as_tibble = FALSE still returns legacy list shape. Standalone wrapper functions Thin, pipe-friendly wrappers around R6 FhirResource methods: fhir_resolve(client, ...) fhir_resolve_batch(client, ...) fhir_resolve_codeable_concept(client, ...) R6 form (client$fhir$resolve(...)) standalone form work; pick whichever reads better pipeline. FHIR client interop helper New exported function omophub_fhir_url(version) returning OMOPHub FHIR Terminology Service base URL given FHIR version ("r4", "r4b", "r5", "r6"). Use httr2, fhircrackr, external FHIR client wants talk OMOPHub’s FHIR endpoint directly.

title: omophub 1.6.0

title: omophub 1.6.0
text: FHIR--OMOP Concept Resolver (client$fhir): Translate FHIR coded values OMOP standard concepts, CDM target tables, optional Phoebe recommendations single API call. resolve(): Resolve single FHIR Coding (system URI + code) text-input via semantic search fallback. Returns standard concept, target CDM table, domain alignment check, optional mapping quality signal. resolve_batch(): Batch-resolve 100 FHIR codings per request inline per-item error reporting. resolve_codeable_concept(): Resolve FHIR CodeableConcept multiple codings. Automatically picks best match per OHDSI vocabulary preference (SNOMED > RxNorm > LOINC > CVX > ICD-10). Falls back text field via semantic search coding resolves.

title: omophub 1.6.0
text: Improved test coverage

title: omophub 1.5.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-26

title: omophub 1.5.0
text: Bulk lexical search (bulk_basic()): Execute 50 keyword searches single API call. Supports shared default filters vocabulary, domain, parameters. search identified unique search_id result matching. Maps POST /v1/search/bulk. Bulk semantic search (bulk_semantic()): Execute 25 natural-language searches using neural embeddings single call. Supports per-search similarity thresholds shared defaults. Query length validated 1-500 characters. Maps POST /v1/search/semantic-bulk.

title: omophub 1.4.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-02-23

title: omophub 1.4.0
text: Semantic search (semantic(), semantic_all()): Natural language concept search using neural embeddings. Search clinical intent like “high blood sugar levels” find diabetes-related concepts. Supports filtering vocabulary, domain, standard concept, concept class, minimum similarity threshold. semantic_all() provides automatic pagination progress bar. Similarity search (similar()): Find concepts similar reference concept ID, concept name, natural language query. Three algorithm options: 'semantic' (neural embeddings), 'lexical' (string matching), 'hybrid' (combined). Configurable similarity threshold optional detailed scores explanations.

title: omophub 1.3.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-01-08

title: omophub 1.3.0
text: Hierarchy get() method: New method retrieve complete concept hierarchy (ancestors descendants) single call support “flat” “graph” visualization formats. Vocabulary domain_stats() method: New method get statistics specific domain within vocabulary. Vocabulary concept_classes() method: New method list available concept classes. Added vocab_release parameter concepts, mappings, relationships, hierarchy methods explicit vocabulary version pinning. Added include_hierarchy parameter concepts$get() concepts$get_by_code().

title: omophub 1.3.0
text: API parameter naming: Updated match OMOPHub API v1.3.0 specifications: limit → page_size pagination vocabulary, domain → vocabulary_ids, domain_ids filtering max_results → page_size result limits relationship_type → relationship_ids relationship filtering target_vocabularies → target_vocabulary (singular) mappings vocabulary_id → vocabulary_ids (plural) hierarchy methods API simplifications: Several methods now fewer parameters match simplified backend API: mappings$get(): Removed direction, include_indirect, standard_only, include_mapping_quality, include_synonyms, include_context, pagination domains$list(): Simplified single include_stats parameter vocabularies$get(): Now takes vocabulary_id vocabularies$domains(): Now takes parameters Batch limits: concepts$batch() maximum reduced 1000 100 concepts per request. Default changes: concepts$batch() standard_only default changed FALSE TRUE relationships$get() default page_size changed 50 100
